_meta.description: Menaion — March 25. The Annunciation of our Most Holy Lady Theotokos and Ever-Virgin Mary.
_meta.source: OCA Department of Liturgical Music and Translations — 2026-0325-texts-matins-yy.docx
notes: One of the 12 Great Feasts (Theotokos feast).
When Annunciation falls on a Lenten weekday: God is the Lord (not Alleluia), but Small Doxology (read, not sung), and Triodion aposticha are included.
Canon heirmoi only — full troparia not included in OCA service text.

troparion.label: Troparion of the Annunciation
troparion.text:
Today is the beginning of our salvation,
the revelation of the eternal mystery!
The Son of God becomes the Son of the Virgin
as Gabriel announces the coming of Grace.
Together with him let us cry to the Theotokos:
"Rejoice, O Full of Grace,
the Lord is with you!"

kontakion.label: Kontakion of the Annunciation
kontakion.text:
O victorious leader of triumphant hosts!
We, your servants delivered from evil
sing our grateful thanks to you, O Theotokos.
As you possess invincible might set us free from every calamity
so that we may sing: "Rejoice, O unwedded Bride!"

magnification._note: Sung at the Polyeleios with selected psalm verses. The magnification refrain alternates with the verses.
magnification.refrain:
With the Archangel's voice
we cry aloud to you, O Pure Virgin:
"Rejoice, O Full of Grace,
for the Lord is with you!"

psalmVerses.text: Give the King Your judgments, O God, and Your righteousness unto the King's son.
He shall come down as rain upon a fleece.

prokeimenon.refrain: From day to day proclaim the salvation of our God!
prokeimenon.verse: Sing to the Lord a new song; sing to the Lord, all the earth!

gospel._note: The Visitation — Mary visits Elizabeth. 'My soul magnifies the Lord…'

postGospelSticheron.author: Cosmas the Monk
postGospelSticheron.text:
Today Gabriel announces the good tidings to her who is full of grace:
"Rejoice, unwedded Maiden!
Be not amazed at my strange appearance, nor be afraid.
I am an Archangel.
The serpent once beguiled Eve,
but now I announce to you the good tidings of joy, O most pure one.
You shall remain inviolate and shall give birth to the Lord."

canon.author: John the Monk
canon._note: Heirmoi only from the OCA text. Full troparia not provided. During Lent, the Triodion canon odes are also sung alongside the feast canon.

ode1.irmos:
I will open my mouth, and it will be filled with the Spirit,
and I will pour forth my words to the Queen and Mother.
I will be seen radiantly keeping feast
and will joyfully praise her conceiving.

ode3.irmos:
As you are a living and abundant spring,
establish, O Theotokos, those who sing your praise,
forming a spiritual chorus;
and in your divine glory
count them worthy of crowns of glory.
ode3._note: On Ode 3, the Biblical Canticle is NOT SUNG; Triodion verses are sung with refrain 'Glory to You, our God, glory to You!'

ode4.irmos:
He Who sits in glory on the throne of the Godhead,
Jesus, the transcendent God, has come in a light cloud,
and by His immaculate hand has saved those who cry:
"Glory to Your Power, O Christ!"

ode5.irmos:
All things were amazed at your divine glory,
for you, O Virgin, who have not known wedlock,
have held in your womb God Who is above all,
and you gave birth to a timeless Son,
Who bestows salvation upon all who sing your praises.

ode6.irmos:
Prefiguring the three days' burial,
the Prophet Jonah in the whale cried out in prayer:
"Deliver me from corruption, O Jesus, King of Hosts!"

ode7.irmos:
The Godly-minded Children
would not worship the creation rather than the Creator,
but bravely trampling on the threat of fire, they rejoiced and sang:
"Blessed are You and praised above all, O Lord God of our Fathers!"

ode8.irmos:
Hear, O Maiden, pure Virgin:
let Gabriel tell you the counsel of the Most High which is ancient and true!
Make ready to receive God,
for through you, He Who cannot be contained will dwell among mortals.
Therefore, rejoicing I cry aloud:
"Bless the Lord, all you works of the Lord!"
ode8._note: After the festal Ode 8, the Triodion Ode 8 is sung without Biblical Canticle, with refrain 'Glory to You, our God, glory to You!' Katavasia from Triodion after Ode 8.

ode9.megalynarion:
O earth, announce good tidings of great joy:
O heavens, praise the glory of God!
ode9.irmos:
Since she is a living Ark of God
let no profane hand touch the Theotokos.
But let the lips of believers
unceasingly sing to her,
praising her in joy with the angel's song:
"Rejoice, O Lady, full of grace, the Lord is with you!"
ode9._note: After the festal Ode 9, the Triodion Ode 9 is sung without Biblical Canticle, with refrain 'Glory to You, our God, glory to You!' Katavasia from Triodion after Ode 9.

exapostilaria.text:
The Captain of the Angelic Hosts
was sent by God the Almighty
to the pure Virgin
to announce the good tidings of a strange and secret wonder:
that as a man, God would be born
of her without seed,
fashioning again the whole race of man.
O people, announce the good tidings of the renewal of the world!

The mystery of God from all eternity
is made manifest today:
the Word of God in His mercy becomes the Son of the Virgin,
and Gabriel proclaims the joy of the Annunciation.
Let us with him exclaim to her:
"Rejoice, O Mother of the Lord!"

lauds._note: The Praises are read, not sung (Lenten weekday). 4 stichera of the feast are sung at the appointed psalm verses.

stichera.verse: Praise Him for His mighty deeds; praise Him according to His exceeding greatness!
stichera.text:
From the vault of heaven Gabriel flew down
and came to Nazareth;
and standing before the Virgin Mary, he cried to her: "Rejoice!
You shall conceive a Son, more ancient than Adam,
the Maker of all things and the Deliverer of those who cry to you:
'Rejoice, O pure Virgin!'"

stichera.verse: Praise Him with trumpet sound; praise Him with lute and harp!
Praise Him with timbrel and dance; praise Him with strings and pipe!
stichera.text:
Gabriel brought the Good News from heaven to the Virgin,
and he cried out the greeting, "Rejoice!
You shall conceive Him Who can be contained in you,
yet Whom the world cannot contain;
and you shall be seen to bear within your womb
Him Who shone forth from the Father before the morning star."

stichera.verse: Praise Him with sounding cymbals; praise Him with loud clashing cymbals! Let everything that breathes praise the Lord!
stichera.text:
The coeternal Word of the Father Who is from all eternity,
without being parted from things on high,
now has come among those below,
in His infinite compassion taking pity upon our fall;
He has assumed the poverty of Adam
and taken a form that is alien to Him.

doxastikon.author: Theophanes
doxastikon.text:
The mystery from all eternity is revealed today,
and the Son of God becomes the Son of man,
that, by sharing in what is worse, He may make me share in what is better.
Adam was cheated of old;
he did not become God, as he desired.
Now God becomes man, that He may make Adam God.
Let creation rejoice, let our nature exult;
for the Archangel stands in awe before the Virgin
and brings the greeting "Rejoice!" to take the place of Eve's sorrow.
In You merciful compassion, O our God, You became man,
glory to You!

aposticha._note: On Lenten weekdays, the aposticha come from the Triodion (for the day) with the feast Glory/Now. These Triodion stichera belong in a triodion file; the feast's contribution is only the Glory/Now.

glory.author: John the Monk
glory.text:
Let the heavens be glad and let the earth rejoice:
For the Son Who is co-eternal with the Father,
sharing His throne, and equally without beginning, has submitted Himself to emptying in His compassion and merciful love for mankind,
according to the good pleasure and the counsel of the Father;
and He has gone to dwell in a Virgin's womb, sanctified by the Spirit.
Oh, wonder! God is come among men!
He Who cannot be contained is contained in a womb;
the Timeless enters time, and oh, great mystery:
His conception is without seed, His emptying past telling!
So great is this mystery!
For God empties Himself, takes flesh, and is fashioned as a Creature,
when the angel tells the pure Virgin of her conception:
"Rejoice, O Full of grace! The Lord Who has great mercy is with thee!"

vespers._note: Vespers texts for the Annunciation are not included in this source document. To be populated from the Vespers service text when available.

troparion.label: Troparion of the Annunciation
troparion.text:
Today is the beginning of our salvation,

Together with him let us cry to the Theotokos:
"Rejoice, O Full of Grace,
the Lord is with you!"
